title: SAML Protocol
author: Keith Richards
summary: A tool for Identity Federation Security Assertion Markup Language, or SAML, is an open standard for exchanging authentication and authorization data between two parties. It’s a common strategy for single sign-on (SSO), allowing users to sign in once and authenticate with multiple third party applications. Similar to OAuth2.0, SAML promotes data security by preventing direct […] The post SAML Protocol…

title: OpenID Connect
author: Keith Richards
summary: Adding AuthN to OAuth2.0 OpenID Connect (OIDC) is an authentication protocol that sits on top of the OAuth2.0 protocol. It provides a standardized way for clients to authenticate users and obtain information about their identity. In simple terms, OpenID Connect allows users to log in to different applications using a single set of credentials. It […] The post OpenID Connect…

title: What is OAuth?
author: Keith Richards
summary: A primer on delegated access OAuth, short for “open authorization”, is a widely used protocol that allows users to grant third-party websites or applications access to their personal information on other websites. It provides a mechanism for secure access delegation across the internet. History Class Prior to the creation of OAuth, sharing of information with […] The post What is…

author: Keith Richards
summary: Demystifying authentication and authorization When you hear the term Auth, what comes to mind? title: Securing Saved-password Applications
author: Alex Smolen
summary: The password is both a ubiquitous and brittle security mechanism.
